title: Login failures spike, TLS handshake errors on auth-service
summary: Users cannot log in across web and mobile. Error rate on auth-service jumped to 64% at midnight UTC. Downstream services report TLS handshake failures when calling auth-service. The identity provider (Okta) looks healthy. Started exactly at 00:00 UTC.
true_root_cause_hidden: The intermediate TLS certificate used by auth-service expired at 2026-06-14T00:00:00Z. Clients and downstream services now reject the handshake (certificate expired). Okta latency and an unrelated rate-limit warning are red herrings; the sharp onset exactly at midnight UTC points at a time-based expiry, not load.

alerts.text: login journey failing: TLS handshake error from 5 regions
IdP token endpoint p95 latency 320ms (slightly elevated)

deploys.change: v2.9.1: dependency bumps, no cert/config changes
deploys.author: release-bot

logs.msg: calling auth-service: x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid: current time 2026-06-14T00:00:03Z is after 2026-06-14T00:00:00Z
TLS handshake error from 10.2.4.9: remote error: tls: bad certificate
leaf cert chain: intermediate 'Internal CA G2' notAfter=2026-06-14T00:00:00Z
rate limit exceeded for client batch (8% of requests)
login check failed: tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate has expired
